contextual_role: Survey/taxonomy evidence for LLM-based agents in software engineering and their memory module categories.
full_text_rationale: Full text is an analytical survey of LLM-based agents in software engineering that filters 115 papers, proposes a perception-memory-action framework, and analyzes semantic, episodic, and procedural memory roles in SE agents; retained as contextual/taxonomic evidence.
extractor_notes: Contextual survey only; do not count as architecture study.
autores: Yanlin Wang, Wanjun Zhong, Yanxian Huang, Ensheng Shi, Min Yang, Jiachi Chen, Hui Li, Yuchi Ma, Qianxiang Wang, Zibin Zheng
titulo: Agents in software engineering: survey, landscape, and vision
resultado_principal: Survey of 115 LLM-based agent studies in software engineering. Proposes a perception-memory-action framework and analyzes semantic, episodic, and procedural memory roles for SE agents.
limitacoes: Secondary study; does not evaluate a new persistence architecture or quantify memory effects directly.
ameacas_validade: Survey filtering and categorization may miss recent or differently named agent systems; extracted taxonomy is contextual evidence, not direct architecture-performance evidence.
